text: PR #112 addresses 11 of 14 audit findings from issue #111 across 11 files. The implementation is clean and correct: blog content validation with ContentSchema, ConsentSchema boolean refinement, EventForm setValueAs conversion, aria-describedby and disabled prop additions across forms, StaffProfileSchema import deduplication, and consent page type fix. Two findings remain partially addressed (EventSeriesForm firstEventDate/capacity missing disabled, beforeunload handler unconditional), and one minor regression of disabled=isSubmitting on org-logo being overridden by the value+onChange pattern.

reasoning: Two previously flagged issues remain: EventSeriesForm firstEventDate (line 304) and capacity (line 328) still lack disabled=isSubmitting, and the setup-profile beforeunload handler fires unconditionally. These are straightforward to fix.

message: Elegant ContentSchema extraction combining .min(1), .max(50000), and HTML-strip refine into a reusable validator shared between CreateBlogPostSchema and UpdateBlogPostSchema. Correctly prevents empty strings and visually-empty rich text.
Replacing z.literal(true) with z.boolean().refine(v => v === true) properly resolves the 'false as never' type hack in consent/page.tsx. Runtime behavior is preserved but types are now correct.
Thorough accessibility fix: all 4 fields receive disabled={isSubmitting}, aria-describedby, aria-invalid, and matching error paragraph id attributes. Screen reader support is now complete.
Disabled styling (opacity-50 cursor-not-allowed) is added to inputCls helper and consistently applied to all 7 form fields with disabled={mutation.isPending}.
Removed inline StaffProfileSchema definition and switched to @/lib/shared import, eliminating schema drift (the inline version was missing department and designation fields).
firstEventDate input does not use the field() helper and still lacks disabled={isSubmitting}. Users can modify this field during form submission, risking race conditions.
suggestion: Add disabled={isSubmitting} to line 304: <input id="firstEventDate" type="date" disabled={isSubmitting} {...register('firstEventDate')} ...

message: capacity input does not use the field() helper and still lacks disabled={isSubmitting}. Users can modify capacity during form submission.
suggestion: Add disabled={isSubmitting} to line 328: <input id="capacity" type="number" disabled={isSubmitting} {...register('capacity', { valueAsNumber: true })} ...

message: beforeunload handler fires unconditionally on all navigation attempts rather than only when formState.isDirty is true. Users get a warning even when no changes were made.
suggestion: Destructure isDirty from useForm's formState and conditionally add/remove the listener: useEffect(() => { const handler = ...; if (isDirty) { window.addEventListener('beforeunload', handler); return () => window.removeEventListener('beforeunload', handler); } }, [isDirty])

message: The checked={field.value as unknown as boolean} cast on the privacy checkbox is now redundant since ConsentSchema uses z.boolean() instead of z.literal(true). Harmless but unnecessary.

Button uses both loading={isSubmitting} and inline text 'Sending...' switch. The Button component already handles loading state — consider removing the conditional children to let the Button's own loading UI handle it.
suggestion: Simplify to: <Button type="submit" loading={isSubmitting} className="px-6"><Send className="h-4 w-4" aria-hidden="true" /> Send message</Button>
